zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert John Morris於1609年1月22日出生在英國貝德福爾斯迪分頓時，他的父親William Morris30歲，母親Lucy Walters55歲。他於大約1637年與Agnes Legate在英國貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1682年10月1日在英國貝德福爾沙恩布盧可享年73歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾沙恩布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert John Morris于1609年1月22日出生在英国貝德福爾斯迪分頓时，他的父亲William Morris30岁，母亲Lucy Walters55岁。他于大约1637年与Agnes Legate在英国貝德福爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1682年10月1日在英国貝德福爾沙恩布盧可享年73岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾沙恩布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: Morris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Mauritz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Armin, Frieda, Helmut, Kurt, Reinhold, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from the personal name Mauritz, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Mauricio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Mario, Roberto, Lilia, Luis, Adela, Andres, Antero, Cipriano.  Portuguese Manoel.
Portuguese (Maurício) and Spanish: from the personal name Maurício (Portuguese), Mauricio (Spanish), derived from the Latin name Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Maurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Denys, Gabrielle, Antoine, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Francois, Gaston, Germain.
French, English, Welsh, Scottish, and Irish: from the Old French personal name Maurice, Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: McMorris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muiris, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of Morris . In some cases this is a Gaelicized form of Fitzmaurice .
Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muirgheasa ‘son of Muirgheas’, a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘sea valor’ or ‘sea choice’. Compare Morrissey .

variants.name: Morici
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Gasper, Aldo, Angelo, Annamarie, Elvio, Giuseppe, Sal, Santo, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a variant of the personal name Maurice (see Morris ).
Hungarian: patronymic from the personal name Móric, Hungarian form of Morris .

variants.name: Moris
meanings: English: variant of   Morris  .

French, Luxembourgish, Flemish, Walloon, North German, and Dutch: from a vernacular form of the French personal name Maurice (see Morris ). In North America, the surname Moris may also be an altered form of French Maurice .
Americanized form of German   Moritz  .

variants.name: Merrick
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meuric, the Middle Welsh form of Maurice (see Morris ).
Irish: borrowed from Welsh. In Ireland, when this is not the Welsh name, it might be adopted for Irish Ó Mearadhaigh, see Merry 2.

variants.name: Maurizio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Marcello, Marco, Mario, Fernando, Matteo, Natale, Sal, Valentino, Vito.
Italian: from the personal name Maurizio, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris 1).

variants.name: Morehouse
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of the many places called Moorhouse in Yorkshire or Durham (from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + hūs ‘house’). In some cases the surname may have become Morris . This name is also established in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert John Morris was born on 22 January 1609, in Stevington, Bedfordshire, England, his father, William Morris, was 30 and his mother, Lucy Walters, was 55. He married Agnes Legate about 1637, in Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 1 October 1682, in Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 73, and was buried in Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, England.

Il a épousé Agnes Legate environ 1637, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 1 octobre 1682, à Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

Oktober 1682 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Sharnbrook, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert John Morris(이)가 1609년 1월 22일 영국 베드퍼드실르 스테빙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Morris(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy Walters(은)는 55세였다. 그는 대략 1637년 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 Agnes Legate(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1682년 10월 1일 영국 베드퍼드실르 샤른브룩에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 샤른브룩에 묻혔다.

Он умер 1 октября 1682 г. в местности шарнбрук, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности шарнбрук, бедфордшир, Англия.
